description: Adversarial invalid lifecycle transition: a Class D conduct artifact whose commit_chain skips from stage 1 directly to stage 3, omitting stage 2 entirely, MUST be rejected as CONDUCT_INVALID. Lifecycle stages MUST be contiguous and monotonically increasing.

input.claim: this strategy is recommended despite a skipped commit-chain stage

why: WiseOrder Protocol v0.1.0 invariants CC2, CC3, CC4: later stages MUST declare dependency on prior stage hashes, missing commit stages MUST invalidate process proof, and commit ordering MUST remain externally auditable. The artifact jumps from stage 1 to stage 3 with no stage 2 emitted. While stage 3's depends_on names stage 1's hash, the stage numbering itself declares a missing intermediate stage — either stage 2 was skipped (CC3 violation: missing commit stages MUST invalidate process proof) or the stage numbering is fabricated (CC4 violation: ordering must be auditable). Mitigates an invalid-lifecycle-transition attack where an attacker compresses required commit stages to bypass conduct surface fields. Status MUST be CONDUCT_INVALID.
